The special blessings God gives us do not protect us from punishment if they don't keep us from sin. They could not expect fellowship with God unless they first sought peace with him. There can be no companionship where there is no friendship. God and people cannot work together unless they agree. Unless we seek his glory, we cannot walk with him. Let us not assume outward privileges without special, sanctifying grace. The warnings in the word of God and his providence against human sin are certain, and clearly show that God’s judgments are near. Nor will God remove the affliction he has sent until it has accomplished its purpose. The evil of sin comes from ourselves; it is our own doing. But the evil of trouble comes from God, and is his doing, whoever the instruments may be. This should encourage us to patiently endure public troubles and to seek to understand God’s meaning in them. The entire passage shows that natural evil, or troubles, not moral evil, or sin, is meant here. The warning given to a heedless world will increase its condemnation another day. Oh, the amazing foolishness of an unbelieving world that will not be moved by the terrors of the Lord and that despises his mercies!

That power which serves as an instrument of unrighteousness will justly be brought down and broken. What is obtained and kept wrongfully will not be kept long. Some are comfortable now, but there will come a day of reckoning, and on that day, everything they are proud of and put confidence in will fail them. God will investigate the sins they have committed in their homes, the robbery they have stored up, and the luxury in which they lived. The splendor and pleasantness of people’s houses do not protect against God's judgments but make suffering more painful and distressing. Yet a remnant, according to the election of grace, will be secured by our great and good Shepherd, as from the jaws of destruction, even in the worst times.
